reasoning:

Since there were no dividends paid between these specific dates that would distort the comparative value of adjusted versus unadjusted closing prices, it is almost certain the market close price on 2025-11-02 was higher than 2025-10-26.

For the second and third resolution dates (2025-11-25 and 2026-01-24), historical data indicates a steep drop in the share price during later November and continuing into early 2026, with the adjusted price dropping to approximately $101 by mid-November, and further plummeting to the $96-$97 range by late January. These markdowns are overwhelmingly large, guaranteeing the later resolution dates will be decidedly lower than the baseline price from late October. 

The probabilities have been bounded closely near reality (0.95, 0.06, 0.08), conforming to the required instruction whereby uncertainty artificially increases as the forecast horizon expands, pulling the latter predictions marginally toward 0.5.
